description: A notorious YouTuber, Salim Wastik, once Salim Khan, has been apprehended by Delhi Police after 26 years on the run. He was convicted for kidnapping and murdering a 13-year-old boy for ransom in 1995. After jumping bail in 2000, he reinvented himself, gaining fame for his controversial online content.

title: 'Quite an evening': Trump wanted 'the show to go on' after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
description: Shots were fired near the White House Correspondents' Dinner venue, prompting President Trump's temporary evacuation as a security precaution. The shooter was apprehended by law enforcement, who acted swiftly and bravely. Trump confirmed the incident and indicated plans to reschedule the event, emphasizing the safety of all attendees.

‘Taken offsite’: Who was the shooter at Trump’s White House dinner? First image emerges
description: President Donald Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after shots were fired, with the suspect apprehended by the Secret Service. An individual reportedly attempted to enter with a gun but was stopped before reaching the ballroom. Trump praised law enforcement and urged the event to continue.

description: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its strong box office run, nearing Rs 1,130 crore in India and Rs 1,780 crore worldwide after 38 days. Despite competition, the film saw significant growth on its 6th Saturday, earning Rs 2.95 crore domestically.  Meanwhile, 'Bhooth Bangla' and 'Michael' also performed on Saturday.

Trump evacuated after shots fired during White House Correspondents' Dinner
description: Panic erupted at the White House Correspondents' Dinner as shots were fired, forcing President Trump and other dignitaries to be evacuated. A 30-year-old Californian man, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was apprehended by the Secret Service after allegedly attempting to breach security with a firearm. An officer was reportedly shot but is expected to recover.

Banda hottest in country at 47.4℃ as UP reels under heatwave
description: Uttar Pradesh is reeling under a severe heatwave, with Banda recording a scorching 47.4 degrees Celsius, its highest in April since 1951. Many districts exceeded 42 degrees Celsius, and the heatwave is expected to persist until Monday. Relief might arrive Tuesday with a western disturbance bringing light rainfall.

title: 'Zoomed camera focusing on legs': Drunk flyer films female crew on Hyderabad–Udaipur flight
description: An IndiGo passenger bound for Udaipur was apprehended at Hyderabad airport for allegedly photographing female cabin crew inappropriately and harassing a co-passenger. The businessman, reportedly intoxicated, was deboarded after ignoring warnings. Police have registered a case under the new penal code for insulting a woman's modesty.

TCS Nashik probe runs into hurdles as accused has different passwords for each file and app
description: Police are facing challenges in a sexual assault and religious coercion probe linked to a Nashik TCS office. The prime accused is using multiple passwords and Face ID on his cellphone, hindering access to crucial digital evidence. Forensic experts are assisting investigators to unlock the device, which is key to the ongoing investigation into nine registered cases.

title: Chhattisgarh village stays awake to see first light bulb glow after decades of isolation
description: For the first time, the remote village of Irapanar in Abujhmarh has electricity, marking a significant arrival after decades of isolation.  Despite challenging terrain, power company teams worked tirelessly to bring light, enabling children to study, mobile phone use, and the introduction of basic appliances. This electrification promises improved access to education, healthcare, and communication for the community.

description: The AAP, as expected, reacted very strongly to this development and accused the BJP of “Operation Lotus”, a term used to define saffron party engineered defections of political rivals. AAP leader Sanjay Singh alleged that central agencies like the Enforcement Directorate (ED) and the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) were misused to pressure and break away AAP leaders. AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal accused the BJP of betraying Punjab, which is ruled by AAP and goes to assembly polls next year.

description: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: After backing Trump's child savings plan, Michael Dell's next $750 m going to hospital
description: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.
